title: eriosema benthamianum
intro: Eriosema benthamianum, also known as the Bentham's Eriosema, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Eriosema benthamianum include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including antioxidant, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Eriosema benthamianum is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and salves, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
